metadata.working_title: BELA: A Fully Automated Blastocyst Evaluation Learning Algorithm for Ploidy Prediction from Time-Lapse Imaging

abstract_sketch.problem: Accurate assessment of human embryo ploidy status is critical for IVF success, but existing methods rely on invasive PGT-A or subjective embryologist grading.
abstract_sketch.gap: Existing image- and video-based deep learning models for ploidy prediction underutilize time-lapse sequences and often require manual embryologist scores.
abstract_sketch.approach: We developed BELA, a two-step multitask BiLSTM pipeline that predicts blastocyst score from day-5 time-lapse sequences (96-112 hpi) and combines it with maternal age for ploidy classification.
abstract_sketch.key_results: BELA achieves AUC of 0.76 for EUP vs ANU and 0.826 for EUP vs CxA on WCM-Embryoscope, matching embryologist-scored models and outperforming day-5 video baselines across four datasets (2,839 WCM + 543 Spain + 869 Florida embryos).
abstract_sketch.impact: BELA removes the need for subjective embryologist annotation, is deployable via a clinical web-app (STORK-V), and provides interpretable quality scores alongside ploidy probabilities.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: IVF has produced over 8 million births globally; embryo selection hinges on ploidy.
PGT-A is the current standard but is invasive, costly, and affected by mosaicism.
Prior automated methods (STORK, ERICA, UBar) rely on single images or limited videos; ERICA reached 70% accuracy and 74% AUC on 1,231 images.
Time-lapse imaging offers richer developmental signals but training on full sequences is noisy.
Contribution: BELA is a fully automated multitask model using only day-5 frames and maternal age.

subsections.title: Training and Validation Datasets
content_bullets: WCM-Embryoscope (1,998 embryos: 494 SA, 588 CxA, 916 EUP, 498 patients).
WCM-Embryoscope+ (841 embryos: 170 SA, 261 CxA, 410 EUP).
Spain / IVI Valencia (543 embryos: 309 ANU, 234 EUP).
Florida / IVF Florida (869 embryos: 202 SA, 222 CxA, 445 EUP).

subsections.title: Blastocyst Score Prediction
content_bullets: Multitask BiLSTM predicts BS, ICM, TE, and expansion jointly.
MAE of 1.855 +/- 0.03 between MDBS and ground truth BS on WCM-Embryoscope.
Multitasking lowers MAE vs non-multitask BELA (1.877 +/- 0.027).

subsections.title: Ploidy Classification on WCM Datasets
content_bullets: BELA EUP vs ANU AUC: 0.66 +/- 0.008 without age; 0.76 +/- 0.002 with age.
BELA EUP vs CxA AUC: 0.708 +/- 0.004 without age; 0.826 +/- 0.004 with age.
Outperforms day-5 video baseline in all scenarios (p < 0.05).

subsections.title: External Validation: Spain and Florida
content_bullets: Spain dataset (543 embryos): BELA beats day-5 video in both with/without age (p < 0.05).
Florida dataset (869 embryos): MDBS correlates more strongly with ploidy (-0.119) than embryologist BS (-0.101).
MAE between MDBS and Weill Cornell re-graded BS (4.16) is lower than vs Florida BS (5.02).

subsections.title: STORK-V Clinical Deployment
content_bullets: Web-based STORK-V platform for clinical use.
Inference for single embryo: 30 +/- 5 seconds.
Logistic regression training: 2.5 +/- 1.2 s; BiLSTM training: 30.3 +/- 11 min.

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: BELA is competitive with embryologist-scored models and surpasses pure video models.
Limitations: ~2,000 sequences, VGG16 best feature extractor, no hormonal data, no mosaic handling.
Clinical implications: reduces cost, avoids unnecessary biopsies, supports patient counseling.

sections.title: Materials and Methods
content_bullets: Temporal/spatial preprocessing: 0.5 hpi intervals, 301 standardized frames, 2h imputation window.
Frames resized from 800x800 to 224x224; Hough Transform segmentation.
VGG16 ImageNet-pretrained extractor -> 512-d feature vectors.
Multitask BiLSTM: 1 bidirectional LSTM + 2 dense, logcosh loss, Adam optimizer.
70/30 split (1,684 after exclusion), 4-fold CV.
Student t-test with Bonferroni correction.
